enrichment.short_description: Pleo issues virtual and physical company cards with expense tracking, approval workflows, and accounting sync for European businesses.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pleo offers business expense cards, spend management, and accounting integrations for startups, SMBs, and enterprises across Europe.

features.title: Virtual & physical cards
features.description: Issue company cards to employees with spending limits set before transactions occur.

features.title: Expense tracking
features.description: Automatically captures and categorizes employee expenses in real time.

features.title: Subscription card management
features.description: Dedicated tools for tracking and controlling recurring vendor subscriptions.

features.title: Accounting integrations
features.description: Syncs transactions with accounting systems including NetSuite, Sage, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 across multiple currencies and entities.

features.title: Approval workflows
features.description: Lets finance teams set spending rules and approvals that apply before a purchase happens.

features.title: Multi-entity management
features.description: The Business plan supports centralized oversight of spending across multiple legal entities.

pros: Every plan includes a free trial
Deep accounting integrations, including NetSuite, Sage, Dynamics 365, Twinfield, and Exact Online
Spending controls are enforced before a transaction happens, not just after
Used by large, recognizable companies such as HelloFresh and Trade Republic
cons: Additional users cost extra on every plan (from €5 to €15 per user/month depending on tier)
Higher-tier features like budgets, advanced approvals, and multi-entity support require the Advanced or Business plan
Business plan starts at €249/month and requires annual billing

faqs.question: Does Pleo offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pleo's pricing page states that all plans include a free trial option.

faqs.question: What accounting software does Pleo integrate with?
faqs.answer: Pleo integrates with systems including Oracle NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics 365, Sage, Twinfield, Exact Online, and Zapier.

faqs.question: Where is Pleo based?
faqs.answer: Pleo is headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark.

faqs.question: Can Pleo manage spending across multiple entities?
faqs.answer: The Business plan adds multi-entity management for organizations operating across several legal entities and currencies.

use_cases.title: Startup expense control
use_cases.description: Small teams issue a limited number of cards with preset credit limits on the Starter plan.

use_cases.title: Mid-market spend management
use_cases.description: Growing companies use the Essential or Advanced plans for expense reports, mileage tracking, and flexible card limits.

use_cases.title: Multi-entity finance oversight
use_cases.description: Enterprises with several legal entities use the Business plan for centralized, multi-currency spend management.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pleo?
paragraphs: Pleo is a business expense management platform that combines company payment cards with expense tracking and accounting integration. It is used by more than 40,000 companies across Europe, including HelloFresh, Soho House, and Trade Republic.
The platform lets finance teams set spending limits and approval rules that apply before a purchase happens, rather than reconciling expenses after the fact.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pleo is for
paragraphs: Pleo serves startups needing basic card and expense control, mid-sized businesses that want centralized expense data, and enterprises that need multi-entity, multi-currency oversight.

enrichment.short_description: Jeeves offers corporate cards, cross-border payments, and expense management for businesses operating across 30+ countries.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jeeves provides corporate cards, global payments, and expense management for businesses operating across Latin America, North America, and Europe.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Physical and virtual cards with real-time tracking and spending controls.

features.title: Global payments
features.description: Transfers to 150+ countries in up to 40 currencies.

features.title: Cross-border payments
features.description: Aims to reduce cross-border payment costs compared to traditional transfer methods.

features.title: Expense management
features.description: Automated tracking, approval workflows, and policy enforcement.

features.title: Stablecoin transfers
features.description: Near-instant cross-border settlement option using stablecoins.

features.title: AI insights
features.description: Predictive analysis and smart alerts on spending.

features.title: Corporate credit
features.description: Non-dilutive credit facilities for businesses.

pros: Supports transfers in up to 40 currencies to 150+ countries
Combines corporate cards, payments, and credit facilities in one platform
Used by recognizable brands such as Porsche, H&M, and Burger King
Offers stablecoin-based transfers for faster cross-border settlement
cons: Pricing isn't published; businesses must contact Jeeves for transaction, cross-border, and financing fee details
No public founding year or headquarters location disclosed on the site
No mobile app or supported UI language list published on the site

faqs.question: What countries does Jeeves support?
faqs.answer: Jeeves states it operates across 30+ countries, including markets in Latin America, North America, the UK, and Europe.

faqs.question: Does Jeeves support stablecoin payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jeeves offers stablecoin transfers as a near-instant option for cross-border payments.

faqs.question: How is Jeeves priced?
faqs.answer: Jeeves does not publish fixed pricing; it charges transactional, cross-border, and/or financing fees that vary by business.

faqs.question: Does Jeeves integrate with accounting systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jeeves integrates with ERP and accounting platforms to support month-end closing processes.

use_cases.title: International expense management
use_cases.description: Businesses with multi-country operations use Jeeves cards and controls to track spend in real time.

use_cases.title: Cross-border vendor payments
use_cases.description: Companies paying international vendors or contractors use Jeeves' multi-currency transfers.

use_cases.title: Access to non-dilutive credit
use_cases.description: Growth-stage companies use Jeeves corporate credit facilities for working capital.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jeeves?
paragraphs: Jeeves is a financial operating system that combines corporate cards, cross-border payments, expense management, and credit into a single platform for businesses operating in 30+ countries across Latin America, North America, the UK, and Europe.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jeeves is for
paragraphs: Jeeves targets companies with international operations that need corporate cards, multi-currency payments, and access to non-dilutive credit, including large brands like Porsche, H&M, and Burger King.

enrichment.short_description: Mesh Payments automates corporate card issuance, expense processing, and AP workflows for enterprise finance teams across 40+ countries.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mesh Payments offers corporate cards, AI-powered expense automation, travel controls, and AP automation for enterprises in 40+ countries.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Virtual and physical cards with policy controls across 40+ countries.

features.title: AI expense processing
features.description: AI-powered receipt processing with automatic GL coding and real-time reconciliation.

features.title: Orchestration layer
features.description: Connects transactions with full context, applying policy checks and budget updates automatically.

features.title: Mia Assistant
features.description: AI-powered employee assistant that handles requests via WhatsApp, Slack, and email.

features.title: Travel controls
features.description: Policy compliance and cost benchmarking for bookings across multiple channels.

features.title: Global operations
features.description: Multi-currency support in 140+ countries.

pros: Combines cards, expense automation, travel, and AP in one orchestration layer
AI assistant (Mia) is reachable via WhatsApp, Slack, and email
SOC 2, PCI DSS, and GDPR compliant with 24/7 human support
Card issuing partnerships span the US, Canada, Europe/UK, and Australia/Singapore
cons: No published pricing; businesses must request a demo for quotes
No founding year disclosed on the site
No dedicated consumer mobile app mentioned on the website

faqs.question: Does Mesh Payments offer an AI assistant?
faqs.answer: Yes, its "Mia" assistant handles employee requests via WhatsApp, Slack, and email.

faqs.question: What compliance certifications does Mesh Payments have?
faqs.answer: The company states it is SOC 2, PCI DSS, and GDPR compliant.

faqs.question: Who issues Mesh Payments cards?
faqs.answer: Cards are issued through partner banks including SoFi Bank (US), Peoples Trust Company (Canada), Adyen (Europe/UK), and NIUM (Australia/Singapore).

faqs.question: Does Mesh Payments integrate with ERP systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers live, continuous sync with ERP and HRIS platforms rather than batch exports.

use_cases.title: Enterprise T&E automation
use_cases.description: Large finance teams with 3,000+ employees reduce manual expense processing and policy violations.

use_cases.title: Corporate travel policy enforcement
use_cases.description: Companies use built-in travel controls for policy compliance and cost benchmarking.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted employee requests
use_cases.description: Employees use the Mia assistant via Slack or WhatsApp for expense-related questions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mesh Payments?
paragraphs: Mesh Payments is a travel and expense management platform for enterprises, automating corporate card issuance, expense processing, travel management, and accounts payable through a connected orchestration layer.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mesh Payments is for
paragraphs: The platform is built for enterprise finance teams, including large organizations with 3,000+ employees, that want to reduce manual expense processing and enforce spending policy in real time.

enrichment.short_description: Float provides Canadian corporate cards, CDIC-insured business accounts, bill pay, and AI-powered expense automation.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Float offers corporate cards, business accounts, bill pay, and AI expense automation built for Canadian businesses, from startups to mid-market.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Unlimited CAD and USD cards with built-in spending controls.

features.title: Business accounts
features.description: CDIC-insured accounts offering interest with no monthly fees.

features.title: Float Intelligence
features.description: AI-powered transaction coding for faster month-end close.

features.title: Bill pay
features.description: Automated accounts payable with free EFT/ACH and wire transfers.

features.title: Reimbursements
features.description: Streamlined employee expense approval and payment processing.

features.title: Foreign exchange
features.description: CAD/USD conversion at rates as low as 0.25% on the Essentials plan.

features.title: Accounting integrations
features.description: Direct connections to QuickBooks, Xero, and (on paid plans) NetSuite.

pros: Free Essentials plan with no monthly fee and up to 1% cashback
CDIC-insured business accounts that earn interest
AI-powered transaction coding (Float Intelligence) on Professional and above
Low FX markup starting at 0.25%
cons: Advanced accounting integration with NetSuite requires the Professional plan or higher
Enterprise plan pricing is custom and requires contacting sales
Physical cards are capped at 20 on the Essentials plan

faqs.question: Does Float have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Essentials plan is $0 per user per month.

faqs.question: What accounting software does Float integrate with?
faqs.answer: Float integrates with QuickBooks, Xero, and, on paid plans, NetSuite.

faqs.question: Where is Float based?
faqs.answer: Float is headquartered in Toronto, Ontario, Canada.

faqs.question: Does Float serve businesses outside Canada?
faqs.answer: Based on the website content, Float is designed specifically for Canadian companies.

use_cases.title: Startup expense management
use_cases.description: Solopreneurs and startups use the free Essentials plan for cards and business accounts.

use_cases.title: Automated month-end close
use_cases.description: Growing teams use Float Intelligence to speed up transaction coding.

use_cases.title: Vendor bill pay
use_cases.description: Companies automate accounts payable with free EFT/ACH transfers.

features: Up to 20 physical and unlimited virtual cards
CDIC-insured accounts earning up to 3.5% interest

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Float Financial?
paragraphs: Float is a financial management platform built for Canadian companies, combining corporate cards, interest-bearing business accounts, bill pay, and reimbursements with accounting sync to QuickBooks, Xero, and NetSuite.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Float is for
paragraphs: Float serves Canadian businesses from solopreneurs and startups to mid-market companies, including non-profits, agencies, e-commerce, and hospitality organizations.

enrichment.short_description: Rho combines business banking, corporate cards, treasury, and automated accounting close with no subscription or per-user fees.
enrichment.starting_price: $0 (no subscription or per-user fees; optional 1% foreign currency fee)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rho offers business banking, cash-back corporate cards, treasury management, and automated close tools with $0 subscription fees.

features.title: Business banking
features.description: Checking and savings accounts with $0 ACH fees and up to $75M FDIC insurance.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Cards earning up to 1.5% cash back on spending.

features.title: Expense management
features.description: Automated receipt capture and reimbursement handling.

features.title: Bill pay
features.description: Vendor payments with zero fees.

features.title: Treasury
features.description: Earn yield on idle cash reserves, managed as a registered investment advisor service.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Send invoices and receive payments directly through Rho.

features.title: Rho Close
features.description: Automated accounting and reconciliation tools.

pros: No subscription costs or per-seat fees for the core platform
Up to $75M in FDIC insurance across banking partners
Up to 1.5% cash back on card spend
Combines banking, cards, treasury, and close automation in one product
cons: Treasury management carries a separate management fee (0.15%-0.6% annually depending on deposit size)
Foreign currency transfers carry a 1% fee
No published founding year or detailed public company information beyond a New York address

faqs.question: Does Rho charge subscription fees?
faqs.answer: No, Rho states there are no per-seat fees or subscription costs for its core banking, card, and expense tools.

faqs.question: How much FDIC insurance does Rho offer?
faqs.answer: Rho states accounts can receive up to $75M in FDIC insurance.

faqs.question: Does Rho offer treasury management?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rho Treasury is a registered investment advisor service with a management fee ranging from 0.15% to 0.6% annually depending on deposit size.

faqs.question: What cash back does Rho offer on cards?
faqs.answer: Rho states its corporate cards earn up to 1.5% cash back on spend.

use_cases.title: Startup banking
use_cases.description: Startups use Rho for fee-free checking and savings accounts.

use_cases.title: Idle cash management
use_cases.description: Companies with cash reserves use Rho Treasury to earn yield.

use_cases.title: Automated close
use_cases.description: Accounting teams use Rho Close to automate reconciliation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Rho?
paragraphs: Rho is a business banking platform that consolidates checking and savings accounts, corporate cards, treasury management, and automated accounting close into one system, with no subscription or per-user fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Rho is for
paragraphs: Rho serves startups, scale-ups, and enterprises that want fee-free banking and cards alongside treasury and automated close tools, including fractional CFOs managing multiple accounts.

enrichment.short_description: Tipalti automates global accounts payable, mass payouts, procurement, and expenses for mid-market and enterprise finance teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tipalti is an AI-powered finance automation platform for AP, global mass payments, procurement, expenses, and treasury operations.

features.title: AP automation
features.description: End-to-end accounts payable with invoice and supplier management.

features.title: Mass payments
features.description: Global payouts to 200+ countries in 120 currencies via 50+ payment methods.

features.title: Procurement
features.description: PO matching and workflow automation.

features.title: Expense management
features.description: Automated expense tracking and submission.

features.title: Treasury management
features.description: Cash flow monitoring and account management.

features.title: Tipalti AI
features.description: Machine learning capabilities for process optimization and insights.

features.title: Multi-entity support
features.description: Centralized management across organizational entities.

features.title: Controls & compliance
features.description: Multi-jurisdictional tax compliance and payment fraud detection.

pros: Supports payouts to 200+ countries in 120 currencies via 50+ payment methods
Pre-built connectors for major ERPs including NetSuite, SAP, Dynamics, and Sage Intacct
Founded in 2010 with an established enterprise track record
Recognized in the 2025 Deloitte Fast 500 (8th consecutive year) and as a 2025 CNBC Top Fintech Company (3rd consecutive year)
cons: Pricing is subscription-based but not published; requires a demo request for a quote
Targeted at mid-market/enterprise, which may be more than very small businesses need

faqs.question: When was Tipalti founded?
faqs.answer: Tipalti was founded in 2010.

faqs.question: Where is Tipalti headquartered?
faqs.answer: Tipalti is headquartered in Foster City, California, USA.

faqs.question: What ERPs does Tipalti integrate with?
faqs.answer: Tipalti has pre-built connectors for Oracle NetSuite, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, and Sage Intacct, among others.

faqs.question: How many countries can Tipalti pay out to?
faqs.answer: Tipalti supports mass payouts to 200+ countries in 120 currencies.

use_cases.title: Global supplier payouts
use_cases.description: Companies with international supplier networks use Tipalti's mass payments.

use_cases.title: Multi-entity finance operations
use_cases.description: Enterprises with multiple legal entities use Tipalti for centralized AP and procurement.

use_cases.title: Marketplace and creator payouts
use_cases.description: Platforms paying large numbers of payees use Tipalti's mass payment methods.
use_cases.audience: Marketplaces and platforms with royalty or creator payouts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tipalti?
paragraphs: Tipalti is an AI-powered finance automation platform, founded in 2010 and headquartered in Foster City, California, that handles global accounts payable, mass payments, procurement, expenses, and treasury operations for mid-market and enterprise organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tipalti is for
paragraphs: Tipalti serves mid-market to enterprise organizations across industries such as adtech, e-commerce, healthcare, manufacturing, nonprofits, and software, particularly those paying suppliers or creators in many countries and currencies.

enrichment.short_description: AvidXchange automates accounts payable — invoice capture, coding, approvals, and payments — for mid-market industries like real estate and construction.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AvidXchange automates AP invoice processing, approvals, and payments with AI, serving real estate, construction, healthcare, and more.

features.title: AI-powered invoice automation
features.description: Reduces AP processing time and improves accuracy.

features.title: Invoice capture and coding
features.description: Digitizes paper-based invoice workflows.

features.title: Payment automation
features.description: Multiple payment options including virtual card, ACH, and checks.

features.title: Approval workflows
features.description: Customizable, multi-step invoice approval processes.

features.title: Real-time visibility
features.description: Reporting and status tracking across AP workflows.

features.title: Supplier network
features.description: Built on 25+ years of transaction data.

features.title: Lien waiver management
features.description: Lien waiver tracking and analytics for construction customers.

pros: Over 200 integrations, including NetSuite, QuickBooks, Sage, and a Workday integration
Serves specialized verticals like construction (lien waivers) and real estate (Yardi, MRI)
Backed by 25+ years of supplier transaction data
Multiple payment methods for suppliers (virtual card, ACH, checks)
cons: No published pricing; requires a demo request for a quote
No founding year disclosed on the site
Primarily built for mid-market/enterprise AP teams rather than very small businesses

faqs.question: Where is AvidXchange headquartered?
faqs.answer: AvidXchange is headquartered in Charlotte, NC.

faqs.question: What industries does AvidXchange serve?
faqs.answer: It serves real estate, construction, healthcare, nonprofits, government, financial services, higher education, and hospitality.

faqs.question: What accounting/ERP systems does AvidXchange integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with 200+ systems including Oracle NetSuite, QuickBooks, Sage, Acumatica, Yardi, MRI, and Workday.

faqs.question: Does AvidXchange handle lien waivers?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers lien waiver management and analytics, aimed at construction customers.

use_cases.title: Construction AP and lien waivers
use_cases.description: Construction finance teams manage supplier payments and lien waiver documentation.

use_cases.title: Real estate AP automation
use_cases.description: Property managers automate invoice processing with integrations like Yardi and MRI.
use_cases.audience: Real estate and property management firms

use_cases.title: Nonprofit and government AP
use_cases.description: Organizations use AvidXchange for approval workflows and reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is AvidXchange?
paragraphs: AvidXchange is an accounts payable automation platform, headquartered in Charlotte, NC, that digitizes invoice capture, coding, approvals, and supplier payments using AI, backed by 200+ accounting and ERP integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who AvidXchange is for
paragraphs: AvidXchange serves finance teams across real estate, construction, healthcare, nonprofit, government, and hospitality organizations, as well as ERP and accounting system providers embedding its AP tools.

enrichment.short_description: Melio lets businesses pay vendor bills by card, ACH, or international transfer, and collect customer payments in one platform.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Melio is a bill pay and invoicing platform for paying vendors and collecting customer payments, with AI tools and QuickBooks/Xero sync.

features.title: Accounts payable
features.description: Pay vendors via credit card, ACH bank transfer, or international payment.

features.title: Accounts receivable
features.description: Create invoices and custom payment links for receiving payments.

features.title: Agent Mel
features.description: AI tool that provides instant answers about bills and payments.

features.title: Automated bill capture
features.description: Capture bills via camera, email, or manual entry.

features.title: Batch payments & approvals
features.description: Workflow automation with team permission management.

features.title: Tax management
features.description: W-9 collection and 1099 data sync.

features.title: Pay-over-time
features.description: Installment payments spread over up to 12 months.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: Manage bill payments on the go.

pros: Free 30-day trial
Processes $100B+ annually across 100,000+ businesses, per company figures
AI assistant (Agent Mel) answers questions about bills and payments
Integrates with QuickBooks Online/Desktop, Xero, and NetSuite
cons: Specific transaction rates are not disclosed on the homepage
High-volume "Platinum" tier requires custom/contact pricing
No public founding year or single headquarters address disclosed

faqs.question: Does Melio offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Melio offers a free 30-day trial.

faqs.question: What accounting software does Melio integrate with?
faqs.answer: Melio integrates with QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Desktop, Xero, and NetSuite.

faqs.question: Does Melio have an AI assistant?
faqs.answer: Yes, "Agent Mel" provides instant answers about bills and payments.

faqs.question: How many businesses use Melio?
faqs.answer: Melio states it serves 100,000+ businesses and processes $100B+ in payments annually.

use_cases.title: Vendor bill payment
use_cases.description: Small businesses pay vendors via card, ACH, or international transfer.

use_cases.title: Accounting firm bill pay
use_cases.description: Firms manage bill payment workflows for multiple clients.
use_cases.audience: Accounting firms and wealth management advisors

use_cases.title: Customer invoicing
use_cases.description: Businesses create custom payment links to collect payments from customers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Melio?
paragraphs: Melio is a bill payment and invoicing platform that lets businesses pay vendors by credit card, ACH, or international transfer, and collect customer payments through custom payment links, with a team of over 600 people across New York, Denver, and Tel Aviv.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Melio is for
paragraphs: Melio serves small businesses through enterprise organizations, as well as accounting firms and wealth management advisors managing bill payments across industries like construction, retail, and healthcare.

enrichment.short_description: Payoneer is a global payment platform for freelancers, businesses, and marketplaces to send and receive cross-border payments in 70 currencies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Payoneer offers multi-currency accounts, mass payouts, and commercial cards for freelancers, businesses, and marketplaces in 190+ countries.

features.title: Multi-currency accounts
features.description: Supports 70 currencies across 190+ countries.

features.title: Payment collection
features.description: Collect payments from clients and marketplaces globally.

features.title: Workforce management
features.description: Hire and pay teams across 160+ countries.

features.title: Commercial cards
features.description: Mastercard-based cards for business expenses.

features.title: Mass payout platform
features.description: Distribute payouts to marketplace sellers.

features.title: Working capital advance
features.description: Business funding option for eligible customers.

features.title: Tax form collection
features.description: Collects 1099, W-9, and 1042 forms.

features.title: Stablecoin payments
features.description: Beta feature enabled via a partnership with Bridge.

pros: Supports 70 currencies across 190+ countries
Combines payment collection, mass payouts, cards, and working capital in one platform
Integrates directly with major marketplaces like eBay, Fiverr, and Upwork
Founded in 2005 with an established track record in cross-border payments
cons: Specific fee and pricing details are not disclosed on the main site and require checking a separate pricing page
Stablecoin payments are still in beta
No public company size or supported UI language list disclosed

faqs.question: When was Payoneer founded?
faqs.answer: Payoneer was founded in 2005.

faqs.question: Where is Payoneer headquartered?
faqs.answer: Payoneer is headquartered in New York City.

faqs.question: What marketplaces integrate with Payoneer?
faqs.answer: Payoneer integrates with marketplaces including eBay, Fiverr, and Upwork.

faqs.question: Does Payoneer support stablecoin payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, as a beta feature through a partnership with Bridge.

use_cases.title: Freelancer payments
use_cases.description: Independent contractors receive payments from international clients and marketplaces.

use_cases.title: Marketplace payouts
use_cases.description: Platforms distribute payouts to sellers or service providers worldwide.

use_cases.title: Global workforce payments
use_cases.description: Businesses hire and pay remote teams across 160+ countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Payoneer?
paragraphs: Payoneer, founded in 2005 and headquartered in New York City, is a global payment platform that lets individuals and businesses send, receive, and manage money across 190+ countries in 70 currencies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Payoneer is for
paragraphs: Payoneer serves freelancers receiving international client payments, businesses managing cross-border payments and remote teams, and marketplaces distributing payouts to sellers.

enrichment.short_description: GoCardless automates recurring payment collection via SEPA, UK Direct Debit, and Autogiro, with pay-as-you-go pricing and 350+ integrations.
enrichment.starting_price: 1% + 20p per transaction (capped at £4 for domestic UK payments, Standard plan)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GoCardless collects recurring and one-off payments via Direct Debit across Europe, with pay-as-you-go pricing from 1% + 20p per transaction.

features.title: SEPA Direct Debit collection
features.description: Collect euro payments across 34 SEPA countries.

features.title: Recurring payment automation
features.description: Automates subscription and recurring billing collection.

features.title: Invoice payment processing
features.description: Collects one-off invoice payments via Direct Debit.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Build custom integrations directly against GoCardless.

features.title: 350+ integrations
features.description: Pre-built connections including Xero, QuickBooks, and Zuora.

features.title: Success+
features.description: Add-on that automatically recovers up to 70% of failed payments.

features.title: Protect+
features.description: Add-on fraud protection and chargeback challenge assistance.

pros: Pay-as-you-go pricing with no setup or monthly fees on the Standard plan
350+ integrations with accounting and business platforms
Add-on failed-payment recovery (Success+) and fraud protection (Protect+)
Covers 34 SEPA countries plus UK Direct Debit and Swedish Autogiro
cons: International payment fees are higher than domestic rates across all plans
Branded bank statements (£50/month) and fully custom checkout (£150/month) cost extra
Custom/volume pricing requires £1m+ annual revenue or a sales conversation

faqs.question: How much does GoCardless charge per transaction?
faqs.answer: The Standard plan charges 1% + 20p per transaction, capped at £4 for domestic UK payments.

faqs.question: Where is GoCardless headquartered?
faqs.answer: GoCardless SAS is headquartered in Paris, France.

faqs.question: What payment schemes does GoCardless support?
faqs.answer: It supports SEPA Direct Debit, UK Direct Debit, and Swedish Autogiro, across 34 SEPA countries plus the UK.

faqs.question: Does GoCardless offer failed payment recovery?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Success+ add-on automatically recovers up to 70% of failed payments.

use_cases.title: Subscription billing
use_cases.description: SaaS and subscription businesses automate recurring payment collection.

use_cases.title: Invoice collection
use_cases.description: Service businesses collect one-off invoice payments via Direct Debit.

use_cases.title: Failed payment recovery
use_cases.description: Businesses reduce churn from failed Direct Debit payments using Success+.

features: Capped at £4 for domestic UK payments
Enhanced identity verification with Verified Mandates

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GoCardless?
paragraphs: GoCardless is a payment collection platform, headquartered in Paris, France, that specializes in automated recurring and one-off payments via SEPA Direct Debit, UK Direct Debit, and Swedish Autogiro, with a REST API and 350+ integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GoCardless is for
paragraphs: GoCardless serves businesses of all sizes collecting subscription or recurring payments across European markets, from small businesses on pay-as-you-go pricing to large enterprises on custom volume-based plans.
